AppSubtitle: Safe live monitoring, visual recognition and gentle RAM/CPU optimization
AppExpansion: Adaptive Neural Enhanced Visual Recognition Engine Daemon
AppDescription: ANEVRED stands for Adaptive Neural Enhanced Visual Recognition Engine Daemon: local performance analysis, safe system actions and visual recognition for monitoring and live translation.
SelectedProcessInfo: {0} | PID {1} | CPU {2:0.0}% | RAM {3:0} MB | Commit {4:0} MB | Priority {5} | Status {6}
FeatureSettingsHint: Turn work areas on or off. Disabled areas disappear from the menu, but can be enabled again here.
ScreenTranslationHint: Manual mode: leave auto-refresh off and press the capture hotkey when you want a new translation. Translation runs through Chrome when Chrome supports its built-in Translator API.
SafetyNote: The app uses normal Windows APIs only. System, security, anti-cheat and protected processes are not manipulated.
FooterNote: No kernel drivers, no anti-cheat bypass, no privilege escalation outside normal Windows mechanisms.

ChromePrivacyNotice: Chrome Translator may send recognized text through Chrome translation services depending on Chrome support and language availability.
ToolDescriptionText: ANEVRED monitors local system load, processes, RAM, VRAM, frametime and Star Citizen sessions. Optimizations are intentionally gentle: protected, system, security and anti-cheat processes are left untouched.
ChromeLicenseNotice: ANEVRED does not bundle Google Chrome. Live translation can use a locally installed Chrome through its built-in Translator API. Chrome and Chrome Translator are Google products; usage and data handling follow the Google and Chrome terms as well as the local Chrome settings.
ThirdPartyNotice: Windows, Chrome and other product names belong to their respective rights holders. ANEVRED stores its settings locally and uses normal Windows APIs.
AiInfoText: ANEVRED evaluates local measurements such as CPU, RAM, GPU, VRAM and frametime pressure as well as visible background load. Recommendations are created only when a safe, explainable action can be derived, for example freeing RAM, reviewing background load, or checking power settings.
AiStorageText: When local learning is enabled, ANEVRED stores up to 14 days and at most 7200 short samples in %LOCALAPPDATA%\ANEVRED\learning-history.json. Samples include timestamps, load values, Star Citizen status and up to eight notable process names. In maximum privacy mode, process names are hashed before being stored.
AiReuseText: This history is loaded again on the next start and used locally only to classify recurring load patterns more accurately. It is not sent to servers and does not replace user judgment: protected, system, security and anti-cheat processes remain excluded.
UiDimmingHint: Click-through RGB filter for bright scenes. Limited to the game monitor.
UiColorFilterHint: Experimental Windows display filter. Channel sliders use 50-120% because lower values can make Windows stop applying the filter reliably. It affects the whole display and is restored when disabled or when ANEVRED exits.
PrivacyDataText: UI dimming stores only local settings such as enabled state, hotkey, RGB values and strength in %LOCALAPPDATA%\ANEVRED\settings.json. Automatic tuning samples the current game image in memory and does not persist screenshots. Live translation captures only the selected screen region, reads text with OCR, and keeps the last OCR/translation result only in memory during the current app session. The ANEVRED-owned Chrome translation profile is cleaned when translation starts and when ANEVRED exits. When local learning is enabled, ANEVRED stores up to 14 days and at most 7200 short samples in learning-history.json; these include performance values and optional process names, but no screenshots.
PrivacySensitiveText: If the selected region shows logins, passwords, tokens, private chats, emails or other confidential data, that data can appear in OCR text, translation output, or shared diagnostic/screenshot files. Select only the needed area, disable translation on login or payment pages, and do not share debug files, logs or screenshots without checking them first.
PrivacyDebugText: OCR debug screenshots are disabled by default. They are saved only when the diagnostic environment variable ANEVRED_SAVE_OCR_DEBUG=1 is intentionally set. In that case files may appear for up to 14 days under %LOCALAPPDATA%\ANEVRED\OcrDebug and can contain sensitive screen content.
DataRetentionSummary: Work data is kept for at most {0}. Settings are preserved.
DataRetentionSummaryMaxPrivacy: Work data is kept for at most {0}. Privacy Maximum limits retention choices to session or 24 hours; settings are preserved.
DataRetentionMaxPrivacyHint: Privacy Maximum is active: only current session or 24 hours are allowed. Turn Privacy Maximum off to use 7, 14, or 30 days.
DataRetentionFullOptionsHint: All retention periods are available: session, 24 hours, 7 days, 14 days, or 30 days.
Name: Name
DeleteWorkDataConfirmText: ANEVRED will delete learning history, session history, exported logs, OCR debug data, and temporary translation data. Settings are preserved.
DeleteAllDataConfirmText: ANEVRED will delete all work data, debug data, exported logs, temporary profiles, and backup/cache folders. Settings are preserved.
LogDataRetentionCleanup: Privacy cleanup: removed {0} old work data item(s).
LogDataStorageDeleted: Privacy: deleted {0} stored work data item(s).
LogMemoryOptimizedProcess: RAM relieved: {0} (PID {1}) working set {2:0} -> {3:0} MB, approx. {4:0} MB freed.
StarCitizenHubSubtitle: Live overview, session history, and shader cache maintenance for Star Citizen
AutomaticDetectionHint: Stutter is detected from frametime spikes. Pressure is detected from high RAM, VRAM, or Star Citizen commit usage.
IssuePatternHint: Automatic stutter and pressure spikes are counted per session and shown in session history.
ShaderCacheManagerHint: Star Citizen cache maintenance for patches, driver updates, and stutter troubleshooting
HardwareMonitorSubtitle: Sensor console for temperatures, clocks, memory pressure, power, and live trends
TemperaturesCoolingHint: ANEVRED displays temperature values available through Windows and graphics drivers. Additional CPU, hotspot, and fan sensors require a supported sensor source.
PowerClocksHint: Power, voltage, clock speed, and throttle data appear when the installed hardware and driver interfaces provide them.
SupportDevelopmentText: ANEVRED is free and developed in spare time. Every contribution helps fund OCR improvements, Star Citizen integrations, testing, and future releases.
HardwareDataInitializing: CPU, RAM, and GPU data is initializing.
HardwareCollectingBaseline: ANEVRED is collecting a baseline. Trends appear after a few seconds.
HardwareHighRamInsight: RAM pressure is above the automatic optimization threshold. Consider closing heavy background apps or using Free RAM.
HardwareHighCpuInsight: CPU pressure is above the profile threshold. Ease CPU can reduce background load.
HardwareHighVramInsight: VRAM pressure is high. Lower the texture cache or restart the game if stutter appears.
HardwareFrametimeInsight: Frame pacing looks uneven. Check overlays, browser tabs, shader cache, and background capture tools.
HardwareStableInsight: No critical hardware pressure detected. The system looks ready for gaming.
ShaderCacheNotFoundHint: No Star Citizen shader cache found. Select the Star Citizen folder or start the game once.
ShaderCacheClearHint: Clear removes detected cache folders without creating a backup. Close Star Citizen before clearing or restoring cache files.
LogShaderCacheCleared: Shader cache cleared ({0} folder(s)). No backup was created.
RegionSelectionInstruction: Hold the left mouse button and drag to select an area. Right-click or press Esc to cancel.

SessionHistoryAfterClose: Session history will appear after Star Citizen closes.
